COMPENDIUM.effect: Lets Link jump, clearing one-tile gaps and hopping over hazards, spikes, and small pits. With a Pegasus Seed active the jump reaches farther.
COMPENDIUM.where: Dungeon item of the 3rd dungeon, Poison Moth's Lair (reached via Spool Swamp / the Tarm Ruins area).

COMPENDIUM.name: Roc's Cape
COMPENDIUM.effect: An upgrade to Roc's Feather. Hold the button after jumping to glide and cross much wider gaps, and chain a double-jump. Replaces the Feather in your inventory.
COMPENDIUM.where: Dungeon item of the 7th dungeon, the Explorer's Crypt (on the Western Coast).

COMPENDIUM.name: Magnetic Gloves
COMPENDIUM.effect: Generate a magnetic field (switchable N/S polarity) to pull yourself toward, or push away from, large metal blocks and balls — and to fling magnetic objects. Used for crossing gaps and solving magnet puzzles.
COMPENDIUM.where: Dungeon item of the 5th dungeon, Unicorn's Cave (in the Sunken City / North Horon).

COMPENDIUM.name: Magical Boomerang
COMPENDIUM.effect: A steerable boomerang that stuns enemies, grabs distant items, cuts grass/bushes, and flips switches. Unlike the basic boomerang you can aim its flight with the D-pad, and it travels farther and hits harder.
COMPENDIUM.where: Dungeon item of the 6th dungeon, the Ancient Ruins (on Goron Mountain). (A weaker, plain Boomerang is a separate reward from the Subrosian Dance Hall.)

COMPENDIUM.name: Bombs
COMPENDIUM.effect: Throwable explosives that blow open cracked walls, destroy certain blocks, and damage enemies. You carry a limited stock that refills from grass, pots, and enemies; a Bomb Bag upgrade raises the carry limit.
COMPENDIUM.where: First found as a chest in the 1st dungeon, Gnarled Root Dungeon; also buyable at Stockwell's shop in Horon Village. The Bomb Bag upgrade (to 30) is earned at the Goron/Tokay feeding minigame.

COMPENDIUM.name: Shovel
COMPENDIUM.effect: Dig in soft ground to clear snow and dirt piles, uncover buried stairs and items, and occasionally turn up rupees or hearts. Essential for several overworld paths, especially in Winter.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by Holly at her house in the Woods of Winter — make it Winter, drop down her chimney through the snowdrift, and she rewards you with the Shovel for clearing her walkway.

COMPENDIUM.name: Flute (Ricky / Dimitri / Moosh)
COMPENDIUM.effect: Call your animal companion to ride. The Strange Flute belongs to one specific animal — Ricky the kangaroo (boxing + high jumps), Dimitri the Dodongo (swims and eats enemies; can be thrown with the Power Bracelet), or Moosh the winged bear (hovers/flies and ground-pounds) — and that's who joins you.
COMPENDIUM.where: WHICH companion you get is set by HOW you obtain the flute: buy the Strange Flute at the Horon Village shop → Moosh; win the Subrosian Dance Hall minigame → Dimitri (then free him in Spool Swamp); do neither, and after beating Blaino and returning Ricky's Gloves you get Ricky's Flute by default.

COMPENDIUM.name: Ring Box
COMPENDIUM.effect: Holds the Magic Rings you can wear at once. The starter box carries one ring; the L-2 box holds three and the L-3 box holds five, letting you swap loadouts.
COMPENDIUM.where: The first Ring Box is given by Vasu at the jeweler's shop in Horon Village; larger boxes come later (the L-3 box via the linked/secret system).

COMPENDIUM.name: Essences of Nature (8)
COMPENDIUM.effect: The eight sacred essences the Maku Tree sends you to collect — one guarded by each dungeon's boss. Gathering all eight grows the Maku Tree's seed and opens the path to confront General Onox. In order: Fertile Soil, Gift of Time, Bright Sun, Soothing Rain, Nurturing Warmth, Blowing Wind, Seed of Life, Changing Seasons.
COMPENDIUM.where: One essence is the reward for clearing each of the eight dungeons, from Gnarled Root Dungeon through the Sword & Shield Maze.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gnarled Key
COMPENDIUM.effect: Unlocks the entrance to the very first dungeon, the Gnarled Root Dungeon, starting your hunt for the eight Essences.
COMPENDIUM.where: Given by the Maku Tree in Horon Village after you first wake it and it tasks you with collecting the essences.

COMPENDIUM.name: Dungeon Keys (Small Keys & Boss Keys)
COMPENDIUM.effect: Small Keys open locked doors within a single dungeon; the ornate Boss Key unlocks that dungeon's boss room. Both are used only inside the dungeon where they're found. Several dungeons also need a special overworld access item (e.g. the Dragon Key for the Dancing Dragon Dungeon).
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests and on defeated enemies throughout each dungeon; they do not carry over between dungeons.

COMPENDIUM.name: Maku Seed
COMPENDIUM.effect: The blessed seed the Maku Tree produces once all eight Essences of Nature are gathered. It's the key that grants you safe passage to Onox's Castle for the final showdown.
COMPENDIUM.where: Produced by the Maku Tree in Horon Village after you deliver all eight essences.

COMPENDIUM.name: Magic Ring
COMPENDIUM.effect: Collectible rings (64 in all across the Oracle games) that grant a passive power while worn in your Ring Box. Found rings appear as unidentified 'Unknown' rings — you must take them to Vasu's jeweler to appraise them (20 rupees) before they can be equipped. Effects range from combat boosts to extra rupees/hearts, immunities, and joke/curse rings.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests, buried spots, Gasha Nuts, enemy drops, and minigames across Holodrum; appraised and managed at Vasu's shop in Horon Village.

COMPENDIUM.name: Power Ring (L-1 / L-2 / L-3)
COMPENDIUM.effect: A notable Magic Ring that raises the damage your sword deals — but raises the damage you take by the same amount (+1 per level at L-1/L-2/L-3). A high-risk, high-reward offensive ring; the L-3 version is obtainable only in a linked game.
COMPENDIUM.where: An appraisable ring (after Vasu identifies it). The L-1 is found inside the Explorer's Crypt; the levels are found separately, with L-3 exclusive to a linked Seasons game.

COMPENDIUM.name: Armor Ring (L-1 / L-2 / L-3)
COMPENDIUM.effect: The defensive counterpart to the Power Ring: it reduces the damage enemies deal to you — but weakens your own sword by the same amount (−1 per level). Good for surviving tough fights when you can afford slower kills; the L-3 version exists only in a linked game (Ages).
COMPENDIUM.where: An appraisable ring (after Vasu identifies it). The L-2 is found on Goron Mountain in Oracle of Seasons; obtained from various ring sources around the world.

COMPENDIUM.name: Gasha Seed
COMPENDIUM.effect: Plant a Gasha Seed in a patch of soft soil; as you walk around, a Gasha Tree grows and eventually bears a Gasha Nut. Cut the nut to claim a prize — rupees, Heart Pieces, rare rings, or other items, with better rewards for nuts you've nurtured longer.
COMPENDIUM.where: Found in chests, given by NPCs, and earned at minigames throughout Holodrum; planted in the marked Gasha soil spots dotted across the overworld.

COMPENDIUM.name: Piece of Heart
COMPENDIUM.effect: A fragment of a Heart Container. Collect four pieces to permanently add one full heart to your maximum health.
COMPENDIUM.where: Hidden across Holodrum — in caves, on ledges, as minigame and sidequest rewards, and inside Gasha Nuts.

COMPENDIUM.name: Heart Container
COMPENDIUM.effect: Permanently increases your maximum life by one full heart and refills your health when claimed.
COMPENDIUM.where: Awarded for defeating each dungeon's boss; four Pieces of Heart also combine into one.

COMPENDIUM.name: Rupees
COMPENDIUM.effect: Holodrum's currency, used at shops to buy bombs, seeds, shields, ring appraisals, and other goods. Green = 1, blue = 5, red = 20, and larger denominations up to 100/200; your wallet's max capacity is raised by upgrades.
COMPENDIUM.where: Earned from grass, pots, dig spots, defeated enemies, chests, minigames, and Gasha Nuts; wallet upgrades found during your travels.

quests.name: Get the Ring Box from Vasu
quests.giver: Vasu — Vasu Jewelers, Horon Village (the ring shop in the southeast of town)
quests.reward: L-1 Ring Box + the free Friendship Ring + ring appraisal service
quests.oneLine: Visit Vasu the jeweler to start collecting, identifying, and wearing the game's equippable magic rings.
quests.how: Walk into Vasu Jewelers in Horon Village and talk to Vasu; the first time you speak to him he gives you the L-1 Ring Box plus your first ring, the Friendship Ring (a cosmetic ring with no effect), and your first appraisal is free. After that, bring him any unappraised Seed Ring you find and he identifies it for 20 Rupees so you can equip it (if you already own that ring, he buys the duplicate for 30 Rupees). Use the menu to pick which appraised ring you have equipped.

quests.name: The magic ring hunt
quests.giver: Found in Gasha Nuts, chests, shops, minigames, and from Maple
quests.reward: Toward the 64 total magic rings (each a small permanent perk)
quests.oneLine: Collect equippable rings — from a damage-boosting Power Ring to handy utility rings — and have Vasu appraise each one.
quests.how: Rings drop from opened Gasha Nuts, hide in chests, appear in shops, are won at minigames, and tumble out of Maple when you bump her. Every new ring is an unappraised Seed Ring until Vasu identifies it (20 Rupees). There are 64 rings in the Oracle ring collection, but it is SHARED across both Oracle of Seasons and Oracle of Ages — a single Seasons file can only find a portion, and completing the set requires trading rings between the two games via linked-game secrets. Many rings are collector trophies; standouts include the Power Ring (more sword damage but you take more damage) and the Armor Ring (the reverse). Only ONE ring can be equipped at a time — the Ring Box level just sets how many you can carry for quick swapping.

quests.name: Upgrade the Ring Box (L-2 and L-3)
quests.giver: L-2: a red Goron on Goron Mountain · L-3: a linked-game secret
quests.reward: L-2 Ring Box (carry 3) and L-3 Ring Box (carry 5)
quests.oneLine: Grow your Ring Box so you can carry more rings for quick swapping — though only one is ever active at a time.
quests.how: The L-2 Ring Box is NOT a Vasu reward — it is given by a red Goron in a cave high on Goron Mountain (bomb the wall one screen east of Goron Village, cross the lava cavern, then drop off the cliff into the cave entrance). The L-3 Ring Box comes only through the linked-game secret system (see the Linked-Game group), so a full-capacity box is a long-game / second-playthrough goal. Important: a bigger box lets you CARRY more rings to swap quickly, but you can still only have one ring equipped/active at any moment.

quests.name: Bumping into Maple the witch
quests.giver: Maple — appears at random on the overworld
quests.reward: Scattered loot: Rupees, Hearts, Bombs, Seeds, Gasha Seeds, Potions, sometimes a Magic Ring (and a one-time Piece of Heart)
quests.oneLine: After enough fighting, the broom-riding witch Maple zooms across the screen — crash into her to scatter her goodies.
quests.how: Maple flies across a screen after you defeat 30 enemies (only 15 if you've appraised and equipped Maple's Ring). Ram her with your sword, shield, or just by running into her so you both spill your items, then race to grab the loot before she does — she grabs the most valuable item first (Piece of Heart, then Gasha Seed, ring, potion, seeds, bombs, hearts, rupees). Her drops are random and can include Seed Rings and, exactly once per save, a Piece of Heart, so chase her down often. Heads-up: depending on the screen, a dropped Heart Piece can land somewhere unreachable — if so, leave and bump her again.

quests.name: Dig up the Star-Shaped Ore
quests.giver: Self-directed (the Subrosia Seaside beach)
quests.reward: Star-Shaped Ore (a trade good)
quests.oneLine: Shovel up a glowing star-shaped chunk of ore buried in Subrosia's red sand to begin a little trade.
quests.how: You reach Subrosia through the warp portals scattered around Holodrum. Take your Shovel to the Subrosia Seaside and dig along the beach until you turn up the Star-Shaped Ore. Hang onto it — the Subrosia Market wants it.

quests.name: Trade for the Ribbon → date Rosa
quests.giver: Subrosia Market, then Rosa the Subrosian
quests.reward: Ribbon → Rosa opens locked doors in Subrosia
quests.oneLine: Swap the Star-Shaped Ore for a Ribbon, give it to Rosa, and she'll unlock Subrosia's sealed doors for you.
quests.how: Take the Star-Shaped Ore to the Subrosia Market and trade it for the Ribbon. Find Rosa and give her the Ribbon — flattered, she goes on a little stroll (a 'date') with you and, while you're together, she opens locked doors in Subrosia that were blocking your path. This is the gate to Subrosian rooms you couldn't reach before.

quests.name: Forge Hard Ore (Red + Blue Ore)
quests.giver: Self-directed → the Great Furnace
quests.location: Subrosia — two ore spots + the Great Furnace
quests.reward: Hard Ore (the shield-upgrade material)
quests.oneLine: Collect a piece of Red Ore and a piece of Blue Ore and smelt them together into Hard Ore at the furnace.
quests.how: Red Ore and Blue Ore each sit in their own spot in Subrosia. Carry both to the Great Furnace and have it smelt them together — the result is a lump of Hard Ore. Take the Hard Ore to the Subrosian Smithy next.

quests.name: Upgrade your shield at the Subrosian Smithy
quests.giver: The Subrosian Smiths
quests.reward: Iron Shield (L-2 shield); Mirror Shield (L-3) only in a linked game
quests.oneLine: Hand the smiths your Hard Ore and they hammer your Wooden Shield into the sturdier Iron Shield.
quests.how: Bring the Hard Ore to the Subrosian Smithy and let them work — they forge your Wooden Shield into the L-2 Iron Shield, which can block stronger attacks. The L-3 Mirror Shield only comes in a linked game: while you already own the Iron Shield, give the smith master the linked 'Smith Secret' (see the Linked-Game group) and he forges the Mirror Shield instead.

quests.name: Win the Subrosian Dance (Boomerang, then a flute)
quests.giver: The Subrosian Dance Hall
quests.reward: 1st win: Boomerang · 2nd win: Strange Flute (becomes Dimitri's Flute)
quests.oneLine: Match the Subrosians' dance steps without bumping anyone to win the Boomerang, then play again for a flute.
quests.how: Step into the Dance Hall and copy the Subrosians' moves to the beat without colliding with a dancer — one wrong step and you're thrown out to restart. Your first clean win gives the Boomerang; win again (the tempo speeds up) and you get the Strange Flute, which becomes Dimitri's Flute IF you haven't already chosen a companion. Win still more times and the prizes drop to Seed Rings, Gasha Seeds, or Ore Chunks.

quests.name: Subrosia's ore-chunk Heart Piece
quests.giver: Subrosia Market
quests.reward: 1 Piece of Heart
quests.oneLine: The Subrosia Market sells a Heart Piece for ore chunks and Ember Seeds.
quests.how: In the Subrosia Market a Piece of Heart is on sale for 20 Ore Chunks plus 10 Ember Seeds (Ore Chunks are Subrosia's currency, dug up around Subrosia with the Shovel). Note: the other 'volcano-eruption' bombable-cave Heart Piece people mention is the Temple Remains one in Holodrum's ruins, reached with Roc's Cape — see the Heart Pieces entry.

quests.name: Choose your companion (the Strange Flute)
quests.giver: Determined by HOW you get the Strange Flute
quests.location: Holodrum (after the Snake's Remains, the first overworld dungeon)
quests.reward: One permanent animal partner: Ricky, Dimitri, or Moosh
quests.oneLine: How you obtain the Strange Flute decides which of three animal buddies is yours for the whole game — you only ever get one.
quests.how: The flute system unlocks after you clear the Snake's Remains dungeon. There are three ways to get the Strange Flute, and the FIRST one you do locks in your partner: BUY it from the Horon Village shop (150 Rupees) → it becomes Moosh's Flute; WIN it from the Subrosian Dance (after the Boomerang) → it becomes Dimitri's Flute; or SCORE 50+ points at the Shooting Gallery minigame → it becomes Ricky's Flute. After you bond with one, you then find that animal in the world and free it from enemies to finish the bond. Once chosen, the other two are gone — pick the ability you want.

quests.name: Ricky the kangaroo
quests.giver: Strange Flute won from the Shooting Gallery (50+ points)
quests.reward: A boxing-kangaroo mount that crosses cliffs and rough ground
quests.oneLine: Ricky punches enemies and his powerful jump clears ledges and gaps that would otherwise block you.
quests.how: To make Ricky your permanent companion, get the Strange Flute by scoring 50 or more points at the Shooting Gallery minigame BEFORE buying the shop flute or winning the dance flute. (Ricky also helps you reach a few spots early in the story regardless of your choice.) Riding Ricky you can punch enemies and use his high jump to scale steep ledges and cross broken terrain. He's the all-rounder for overland exploration.

quests.name: Dimitri the Dodongo
quests.giver: Strange Flute won from the Subrosian Dance
quests.reward: A swimming mount that handles deep water and rapids
quests.oneLine: Dimitri the friendly Dodongo swims, eats enemies, and crosses water and rapids before you have the Flippers.
quests.how: Get the Strange Flute by winning the Subrosian Dance (your second win, after the Boomerang) and it becomes Dimitri's. You then find Dimitri being harassed by the Great Moblin's henchmen in Spool Swamp — pay them off (about 80 Rupees, in two 50 + 30 demands) and he joins you. Dimitri swims across deep water, rides river currents safely, and gobbles enemies. He's the pick for early water mobility.

quests.name: Moosh the flying bear
quests.giver: Strange Flute bought from the Horon Village shop
quests.reward: A hovering bear mount with a ground-pound
quests.oneLine: Moosh the winged bear hovers across gaps and slams the ground to stun enemies and hit floor switches.
quests.how: Buy the Strange Flute from the Horon Village shop (150 Rupees) and it becomes Moosh's Flute; you then find Moosh being attacked by enemies, and clearing them makes him your companion. Riding Moosh you flap to hover over pits and short gaps and perform a heavy ground-pound that stuns nearby enemies and triggers floor switches. He's the choice if you want air time over pits.

quests.name: The Holodrum trading game (Cuccodex → Noble Sword)
quests.giver: Dr. Left, then a chain of villagers across Holodrum and Subrosia
quests.reward: Noble Sword (the L-2 sword — stronger than the Wooden Sword)
quests.oneLine: A long trade chain starting with a Cuccodex and ending in the upgraded Noble Sword.
quests.how: After the first dungeon, light the two torches in Dr. Left's house in Horon with Ember Seeds to get the Cuccodex, then trade it down the chain: Lon Lon Egg (Malon) → Ghastly Doll (Maple) → Iron Pot (Mrs. Ruul) → Lava Soup (the Subrosian Chef) → Goron Vase (Biggoron, atop Goron Mountain) → Fish (Ingo, the vase collector in Sunken City) → Megaphone (the old man in North Horon — the Fish lures his cat down) → Mushroom (Talon, asleep on Mt. Cucco, woken by the Megaphone) → Wooden Bird (Syrup the witch, near Sunken City) → Engine Grease (the clockmaker, Tick Tock's shop in Horon) → Phonograph (Guru-Guru atop the windmill). Finally take the Phonograph to the Deku Scrub in the Lost Woods; play it for him and he tells you how to reach the Noble Sword. Each step needs the right person, so follow the order.

quests.name: Grow Gasha Seeds in soft soil
quests.giver: Self-directed (Gasha Seeds found in chests, from Maple, etc.)
quests.reward: Gasha Nuts (Rupees, rings, potions — and one Piece of Heart)
quests.oneLine: Plant a Gasha Seed in soft soil, kill enemies as you travel, and come back to crack open a fruit full of loot.
quests.how: Find a Gasha Seed (chests, minigames, Maple), then plant it in one of the soft-soil spots dotted around the overworld. As you fight enemies elsewhere the planted nut ripens; return later and slash the grown Gasha Nut open for the goodies inside. Higher-ranked (harder-to-reach) soil patches give better hauls, including the rare one-time Gasha Piece of Heart.

quests.name: Notable Pieces of Heart
quests.giver: Various (minigames, bosses, hidden spots)
quests.reward: Pieces of Heart (4 = one full Heart Container; 12 total in the game)
quests.oneLine: Hunt the optional Heart Pieces to extend your life bar — there are 12 to find across the whole game.
quests.how: There are 12 Pieces of Heart in Oracle of Seasons. Reliable standouts: the Subrosia Market sells one for 20 Ore Chunks + 10 Ember Seeds; beating the Great Moblin at his keep (catch and throw back his bombs) drops one; the Temple Remains hides one in a bombable cave you cross with Roc's Cape; Maple drops a one-time piece; and a high-rank Gasha Nut yields one. Others sit in Horon Village, the Eastern Suburbs, Woods of Winter, Mt. Cucco, Spool Swamp, Eyeglass Lake, and the Graveyard. Collect four of any to earn a full Heart Container.

quests.name: Minigames for rings and rupees
quests.giver: Various game stalls
quests.reward: Rings, Rupees, the Boomerang (Dance Hall), and a companion flute
quests.oneLine: Holodrum's minigames — the Subrosian Dance, the Shooting Gallery, fortune-telling — pay out in rings, gear, and cash.
quests.how: The Subrosian Dance Hall gives the Boomerang, then the Strange Flute (→ Dimitri), then Seed Rings/Gasha Seeds/Ore Chunks on repeat. The Shooting Gallery can hand you the Strange Flute (→ Ricky) at 50+ points. Fortune-telling and shop games can give fresh Seed Rings to take to Vasu. Treat these as the steady, low-stakes way to pad out rings and Rupees between dungeons.

quests.name: Link Seasons to Ages with a password
quests.giver: The game's secret/password system (Farore records your secret)
quests.reward: A continued, harder 'linked' adventure that carries progress between the two Oracles
quests.oneLine: A password earned in one Oracle game unlocks the linked story in the other, tying Seasons and Ages into one tale.
quests.how: Beating one game gives you a long 'Secret' password; entering it when starting the other game begins a Linked Game. In a linked Seasons file you start stronger (a sword and extra hearts), keep your animal companion, and unlock content the standalone game lacks. The password encodes your choices, so write it down exactly — it's the bridge between the two cartridges.

quests.name: The little linked secrets (rings, shield, Ring Box)
quests.giver: NPCs who trade short secret passwords (snakes, smiths, townsfolk)
quests.reward: Mirror Shield (L-3), the L-3 Ring Box, extra rings (e.g. Snowshoe Ring) and more
quests.oneLine: Short secret codes passed between the two games unlock bonus gear like the Mirror Shield and rare rings.
quests.how: In a linked game, certain NPCs give you a short secret in one Oracle to tell to their counterpart in the other. The 'Smith Secret' (from a Subrosian in Ages) upgrades your Iron Shield to the Mirror Shield (L-3) at the Subrosian Smithy. A secret from Symmetry City in Ages earns the L-3 Ring Box. Red/Blue snake secrets transfer your appraised rings between games. Other side-secrets reward unique rings. These are optional but are the only way to a full 64-ring set and the best shield.

quests.name: The true final boss (Twinrova & Ganon)
quests.giver: Story-gated — only in a linked playthrough
quests.location: The Room of Rites (via the Maku Tree)
quests.reward: The complete ending + the Hero's Secret carry-over
quests.oneLine: Only in a linked game does the real villain reveal herself — Twinrova captures Zelda to resurrect Ganon, the true final foe.
quests.how: Finish the SECOND Oracle as a linked game and the plots converge: Twinrova (the witches Koume and Kotake) has been the mastermind, seizing Princess Zelda as a sacrifice to revive Ganon. After your last dungeon the Maku Tree opens the Room of Rites, where you fight Twinrova and then Ganon himself — the true ending the single games never show. Beating it grants the Hero's Secret to start a fresh, stronger file.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Temple of Seasons Great Fairy (Linked-Game secret — NOT a heal)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: In the main chamber where you got the Rod of Seasons, walk UP through the waterfall at the top of the screen into a hidden room. She only appears here AFTER you defeat Onox (late/post-game).
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: NOT a healer (the draft was wrong). She is a Linked-Game secret-keeper: she tells Link a password to carry to a linked Oracle of Ages game to earn the Heart Ring L-1 (and she receives a secret from the Wing Dungeon Great Fairy in Ages). No effect in a single, unlinked playthrough beyond the secret.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Fairy Fountain — Woods of Winter
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Holodrum — Woods of Winter (the snowy region east/northeast of Horon Village)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Directly WEST of the Snake's Remains dungeon (the draft's "south-then-west of the Tree Stump" landmark is wrong). Step into the fountain.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: FREE — a healing fountain: the Great Fairy fully refills Link's hearts on contact, and once awoken you can scoop loose fairies here into an Empty Bottle. No item upgrade.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Fairy Fountain — Western Coast
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Holodrum — Western Coast (far southwest, the shipwreck beach by Hero's Cave)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Inside a CAVE just NORTHWEST of the Hero's Cave entrance (the draft's "one screen due west" is imprecise — it's a cave to the northwest). Enter and step into the fountain.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: FREE — a healing fountain: fully restores Link's hearts; loose fairies can be bottled. No item given.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Fairy Fountain — North Horon / Natzu (by the river)
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Holodrum — North Horon, in the Natzu animal-companion area (center of the map)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Near the river in North Horon. Note: this area changes shape with your animal companion (Ricky/Dimitri/Moosh), so the exact approach varies; the riverside fountain is the reliable one. (The draft's "cave in the southwest of Natzu" is not confirmed — softened.)
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: FREE — a healing fountain: refills all of Link's hearts; loose fairies can be bottled. No item given.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Other healing fairy fountains (Holodrum has SIX total)
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Holodrum — Mt. Cucco · Tarm Ruins · Samasa Desert (plus the three above)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Beyond the three above, three more healing fountains exist: Mt. Cucco (in an upper cave reachable only in Winter), Tarm Ruins (bomb the wall near the Lost Woods to open it), and Samasa Desert (behind a blocked wall, in the area with the skull marker). Exact step counts vary by source, so treat these as "a fountain is here," not turn-by-turn.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: FREE — all are healing fountains (full heart refill + bottle-able fairies once awoken), same as the others. No item upgrades.

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Subrosian Smithy — Shield Temper (Iron Shield)
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Subrosia (the underground realm reached via portals)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: In western Subrosia. First forge HARD ORE: get the Magnetic Gloves, then use them to pull the Red Ore (eastern Subrosia) and Blue Ore (far-western Subrosia) and fuse the two at the Great Furnace. Bring the resulting Hard Ore plus your Wooden Shield to the two smiths and pick "Do whatever."
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: UPGRADE — tempers your Wooden Shield (L-1) into the Iron Shield (L-2). Requires Hard Ore. (In a Linked Game, bringing an already-Iron Shield can yield the Mirror Shield.)

GREAT_FAIRIES.name: Sword upgrade — Noble Sword (Lost Woods pedestal, single-play)
GREAT_FAIRIES.region: Holodrum — Lost Woods (southwest of Horon Village)
GREAT_FAIRIES.location: Not a fairy — this is the real one-time SWORD upgrade for a normal (unlinked) playthrough. Do the trade chain to get the Phonograph, play it for the Deku/Business Scrub in the Lost Woods to learn the path, then move one screen at a time changing the season at each stump: Winter then go LEFT, Autumn then LEFT, Spring then LEFT, Summer then LEFT (you head west each screen). The sword waits in a pedestal at the end.
GREAT_FAIRIES.cost: UPGRADE — grants the Noble Sword (L-2), stronger than the Wooden Sword; free once you walk the woods correctly. (Completing the trade chain is also what lets Syrup sell Magic Potion.) In a Linked Game, a password secret can instead upgrade the blade to the Noble/Master Sword line — but in single-play the Lost Woods pedestal is the confirmed route.

RUNES.name: Rod of Seasons
RUNES.from: Found in the Temple of Seasons, which has sunk into Subrosia (the under-realm beneath Holodrum). You get the rod itself with NO season power; the four Season Spirits grant the seasons one at a time.
RUNES.what: Your signature tool. Stand on a season stump and swing it to change Holodrum's season — Spring, Summer, Autumn, or Winter — reshaping the land around you (vines grow in Summer, water freezes to walkable ice in Winter, etc.).
RUNES.tip: It only works while standing on a tree-stump. You can't change seasons at all until the Spirit of Winter gives you your first power; you earn the other three by reaching each Season Spirit's tower as the game goes on.

RUNES.name: Spirit of Winter
RUNES.from: Tower of Winter, a corner of the Temple of Seasons in Subrosia, reached right after the first dungeon (Gnarled Root). Winter is the FIRST season power you unlock.
RUNES.what: Adds Winter to the Rod of Seasons. Winter freezes water into ice you can walk across, blankets the land in snow (snowdrifts can become climbable steps), and lowers some water levels — but also covers warm-weather plants.
RUNES.tip: Winter is your bridge-builder: a lake or gap blocking the path often means 'come back and freeze it.'

RUNES.name: Spirit of Summer
RUNES.from: Tower of Summer in the Temple of Seasons, Subrosia.
RUNES.what: Adds Summer to the Rod of Seasons. Summer makes vines flourish (so you can climb some cliffs), dries up certain shallow water, and ripens certain plants.
RUNES.tip: If a cliff has bare climbing spots but no vines, switch to Summer at a nearby stump — Poison Moth's Lair (dungeon 3) is reached exactly this way.

RUNES.name: Spirit of Spring
RUNES.from: Tower of Spring in the Temple of Seasons, Subrosia.
RUNES.what: Adds Spring to the Rod of Seasons. Spring makes flowers bloom — including bouncy flower-buds that launch Link up ledges — and melts the last of the snow.
RUNES.tip: Spring's blooming flowers act like springboards to reach higher ground you can't otherwise climb.

RUNES.name: Spirit of Autumn
RUNES.from: Tower of Autumn in the Temple of Seasons, Subrosia.
RUNES.what: Adds Autumn to the Rod of Seasons. Autumn grows large mushrooms you can lift/push aside or use as platforms, and causes some plants to wither, clearing paths.
RUNES.tip: With all four seasons unlocked you can solve any season puzzle — many areas only open in one specific season, so cycle through them if you're stuck.

RUNES.name: Wooden Sword
RUNES.from: Found in Hero's Cave, on the beach just west/southwest of Horon Village — you need it to prove your courage and reach the Maku Tree. (In a linked game you start already holding it.)
RUNES.what: Your basic blade (L-1). Slashes enemies, cuts grass and bushes, and can fire a sword-beam when you're at full hearts.
RUNES.tip: Hold the button to charge a Spin Attack — useful for crowds and your highest-damage swing.

RUNES.name: Noble Sword
RUNES.from: Hidden deep in the Lost Woods (in Tarm Ruins). Reach it by stepping one screen west at a time, changing to the right season at each stump first — Winter, then Autumn, then Spring, then Summer (a hint the Deku Scrub gives if you play him the Phonograph's song).
RUNES.what: The L-2 sword — twice as strong as the Wooden Sword and the best blade you can get in a normal (non-linked) game.
RUNES.tip: The Lost Woods is a pure season puzzle; follow the season+west sequence exactly and you'll pop out at the sword's pedestal.

RUNES.name: Master Sword
RUNES.from: LINKED GAME ONLY. In a game linked from Oracle of Ages, an old woman gives you a 'Hero's Secret'; take it to the old man hidden behind Horon Village's clock shop (dig out the stairs with the Shovel) and survive his monster wave. If you ALREADY own the Noble Sword it's upgraded to the Master Sword; otherwise the secret only gives you the Noble Sword.
RUNES.what: The L-3 sword and the strongest standard sword — a major damage boost over the Noble Sword.
RUNES.tip: You can't get this in a single solo playthrough; it's a reward for playing both Oracle games, linking them, and already having the Noble Sword.

RUNES.name: Fool's Ore
RUNES.from: Forced on you by the Strange Brothers in Subrosia: when you first arrive they bump into you, your Roc's Feather drops, and they snatch it — shoving this 'treasure' into your hands in trade.
RUNES.what: A strange, heavy ore you can swing like a weapon — surprisingly powerful while you have it.
RUNES.tip: It's TEMPORARY. Trail the brothers through the Treasure Grove WITHOUT being seen; they bury your Roc's Feather in a sand pit, and you dig it back out with the Shovel — at which point the Fool's Ore is discarded. Don't get attached.

RUNES.name: Wooden Shield
RUNES.from: Your starting shield (L-1) — buy it cheaply from the Horon Village shop early on.
RUNES.what: Blocks weak enemy attacks and projectiles when held up.
RUNES.tip: Like-Likes will eat your shield if they grab you — mash buttons to break free before it's swallowed.

RUNES.name: Iron Shield
RUNES.from: The L-2 shield. Take a Wooden Shield plus a piece of Hard Ore to the Subrosian Smithy and they forge it for you.
RUNES.what: A sturdier shield that blocks stronger attacks the Wooden Shield can't.
RUNES.tip: Worth getting as soon as you have Hard Ore; it deflects things that would otherwise chip your hearts.

RUNES.name: Mirror Shield
RUNES.from: The L-3 shield — LINKED GAME ONLY. Tell the Subrosian Smithy the 'Smith Secret' from a linked Oracle of Ages game while you already own the Iron Shield, and they upgrade it.
RUNES.what: The top-tier shield, blocking attacks the lesser shields can't.
RUNES.tip: A linked-game-only reward — in a normal solo playthrough the Iron Shield is the best you'll have.

RUNES.name: Seed Satchel
RUNES.from: The reward inside the first dungeon, Gnarled Root Dungeon — grab it after the mini-boss; it already holds 20 Ember Seeds.
RUNES.what: Holds your magic Seeds and lets you toss them or load them into the Slingshot. Capacity upgrades over the quest so you can carry more of each seed type.
RUNES.tip: The satchel is the engine for half your puzzle-solving — refill it by cutting grass or whacking the matching seed-trees scattered around Holodrum.

RUNES.name: Ember Seeds
RUNES.from: Your first seed type — they come pre-loaded in the Seed Satchel you find in Gnarled Root Dungeon. Refill at any Ember Seed tree (or by cutting grass).
RUNES.what: Burst into flame on impact — light torches, burn away certain bushes/leaves, and damage enemies.
RUNES.tip: The go-to for lighting dark rooms' torches to open doors. Load them in the Slingshot to hit far-off torches.

RUNES.name: Scent Seeds
RUNES.from: Harvested from a Scent Seed tree once you've found one in Holodrum.
RUNES.what: Give off a smell that lures enemies toward where they land; fired from the Slingshot they also deal damage and knock enemies back.
RUNES.tip: Use them to bait monsters off a ledge or onto a switch, or just as straight ammo from the Slingshot.

RUNES.name: Pegasus Seeds
RUNES.from: Collected from a Pegasus Seed tree in Holodrum.
RUNES.what: Sprinkle them to dash at high speed for a short time (like temporary Pegasus Boots). They also briefly stun enemies, and paired with Roc's Feather they make Link jump much farther.
RUNES.tip: Pegasus dash + Roc's Feather is the standard combo for clearing wide gaps.

RUNES.name: Gale Seeds
RUNES.from: Harvested from a Gale Seed tree in Holodrum.
RUNES.what: Summon a whirlwind that warps Link to any seed-tree he has already visited — fast travel across the overworld. They can also blow weaker enemies clean off the screen.
RUNES.tip: Toss one in the open overworld to pop up a tree-warp menu; it's the quickest way to cross Holodrum.

RUNES.name: Mystery Seeds
RUNES.from: Collected from a Mystery Seed tree in Holodrum.
RUNES.what: Used on stone Owl statues to reveal hints; thrown at most enemies they produce a RANDOM effect like one of the other seed types.
RUNES.tip: If a stone owl is staring at you and you're stuck, drop a Mystery Seed on it for a clue.

RUNES.name: Slingshot
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 5, the Dancing Dragon Dungeon.
RUNES.what: Fires whichever Seed you have selected in a straight line, turning your seeds into ranged ammo (great for far-off torches, switches, and enemies).
RUNES.tip: Pair it with Ember Seeds for distant torches and with Scent Seeds for ranged damage.

RUNES.name: Hyper Slingshot
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 8, the Sword & Shield Maze — an upgrade to your Slingshot.
RUNES.what: The upgraded Slingshot — fires THREE seeds at once in a spread, tripling your coverage.
RUNES.tip: The spread makes it far easier to light multiple torches or hit several statues/switches in one shot.

RUNES.name: Power Bracelet
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 2, Snake's Remains.
RUNES.what: Lets Link grab and lift heavy objects — pots, rocks, and bushes — to throw them or clear blocked paths.
RUNES.tip: Once you have it, lots of earlier 'dead ends' (a rock you couldn't move) suddenly open up. Hold to lift, press again to throw.

RUNES.name: Roc's Feather
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 3, Poison Moth's Lair (reached by growing vines with Summer).
RUNES.what: Lets Link JUMP — hop over gaps, spikes, and pits, and dodge attacks. The foundation of nearly every platforming puzzle.
RUNES.tip: Combine with Pegasus Seeds for a long jump to clear wide chasms.

RUNES.name: Roc's Cape
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 7, the Explorer's Crypt — an upgrade that replaces Roc's Feather.
RUNES.what: The upgraded Feather — Link gains a longer hovering jump (tap jump again to flutter/glide), crossing gaps the plain Feather can't.
RUNES.tip: Tap jump a second time at the peak of a hop to float forward; essential for the longest jumps in the late game.

RUNES.name: Magnetic Gloves
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 4, Unicorn's Cave.
RUNES.what: Project a switchable N/S magnetic pole to pull yourself toward (or push off from) big metal blocks and magnetic balls — used to fling Link across rooms and move iron objects.
RUNES.tip: Flip the polarity to attract OR repel; many puzzles hinge on launching yourself off a same-pole magnet.

RUNES.name: Magical Boomerang
RUNES.from: The dungeon item in Dungeon 6, the Ancient Ruins.
RUNES.what: A steerable boomerang — you can guide its flight with the D-pad. Stuns enemies, grabs distant items, cuts grass, and hits remote switches.
RUNES.tip: Because you can curve its path, it reaches switches and pickups a thrown straight boomerang never could.

RUNES.name: Bombs
RUNES.from: First found in a chest in Dungeon 1, Gnarled Root Dungeon; afterward buy refills from shops or find them in grass/enemies.
RUNES.what: Throwable explosives that blow open cracked walls, reveal hidden passages, damage enemies, and trigger some switches. Capacity starts low and can be upgraded (the Big Bomb Bag in Subrosia raises it).
RUNES.tip: Suspicious cracked wall? Set a bomb next to it. Many secrets (and the odd Gasha spot) hide behind blast-able walls.

RUNES.name: Shovel
RUNES.from: Given by Holly at her house in the Woods of Winter (east of Horon Village). Use Winter's snowdrift to reach her chimney, tumble in, and she rewards you for clearing the snow off her walkway.
RUNES.what: Digs up soft soil and clears snowdrifts and dirt piles, uncovering Rupees, Hearts, Ore Chunks, Gasha Seeds, and hidden stairs.
RUNES.tip: It's also the tool you use in Subrosia to dig your Roc's Feather back out from where the Strange Brothers buried it.

RUNES.name: Flute
RUNES.from: Earned by teaming up with one animal companion — Ricky gives you his flute by default, or you instead get Dimitri's or Moosh's flute depending on which Strange Flute you obtain first (see below).
RUNES.what: Summons your chosen animal companion to your side out in the overworld so they can carry you across terrain.
RUNES.tip: You get exactly ONE companion for the whole game, decided by the flute you're holding when you reach Natzu (the Spool Swamp area) — choose deliberately.

RUNES.name: Ricky
RUNES.from: A boxing kangaroo found in North Horon / Holodrum Plain (east then north of Horon Village), being bullied by Blaino. He becomes your companion — and hands you his Flute — if you haven't gotten a Strange Flute first. The 'default' companion.
RUNES.what: Carries Link and punches through enemies and certain barriers; can boxing-jump up and down small ledges to cross rough land.
RUNES.tip: To free him, pay Blaino 10 Rupees, win his boxing match, and return Ricky's stolen gloves. Do nothing special with a Strange Flute and Ricky is who you'll end up with — he's great on land.

RUNES.name: Dimitri
RUNES.from: A friendly Dodongo. To make him your companion, win the Strange Flute at the Subrosian Dance Hall minigame, then rescue him from Moblins in the Spool Swamp/Natzu area (pay them 80 Rupees). (If you never won that flute, you instead just meet him in the Sunken City and he doesn't join.)
RUNES.what: Carries Link and SWIMS — he can cross water, climb up waterfalls, and gulp down almost any enemy. The water-mobility companion, and the only one who can enter the Sunken City.
RUNES.tip: Pick Dimitri if you like exploring lakes and rivers; he ignores currents and eats most foes whole.

RUNES.name: Moosh
RUNES.from: A winged blue bear. Buy the Strange Flute from the Horon Village shop for 150 Rupees, then find Moosh under attack and save him — the Strange Flute becomes Moosh's Flute and he joins you.
RUNES.what: Carries Link and can briefly FLY/hover to glide over pits and gaps, plus a ground-pound that stuns enemies.
RUNES.tip: Moosh's float makes crossing chasms trivial — buy his flute before you reach Natzu/Spool Swamp if you want him.

RUNES.name: Gasha Seed
RUNES.from: Found in chests, dug up with the Shovel, bought from shops, and given as quest/Maku rewards throughout Holodrum.
RUNES.what: Plant one in a patch of Soft Soil; come back later and it grows into a Gasha Tree bearing a Gasha Nut. Crack the nut for prizes.
RUNES.tip: Gasha Nuts often hold magic Rings — plus Magic Potions, fairies, Rupees, or Hearts — so plant every Gasha Seed you find.

RUNES.name: Magic Rings
RUNES.from: Found all over — Gasha Nuts, dungeon chests, dropped by enemies (notably Maple), buried in soil, and from NPCs. Must be appraised by Vasu before use.
RUNES.what: Equippable rings, each granting a passive effect (extra attack, defense, special perks, transformations, novelty effects, and more). You store appraised rings in your Ring Box and equip ONE at a time.
RUNES.tip: Bring unidentified rings to Vasu to learn what they do; a damage or defense ring equipped early makes the whole quest smoother.

RUNES.name: Ring Box (Vasu)
RUNES.from: The L-1 box is given by Vasu the first time you visit his shop in Horon Village; the L-2 box is earned later in the quest, and the L-3 box is LINKED GAME ONLY.
RUNES.what: Stores the appraised Rings you own — L-1 holds 1 ring, L-2 holds 3, L-3 holds 5. (Note: you can only have ONE ring equipped at a time regardless of box size; the box just sets how many you can keep ready to swap.) Vasu also appraises new rings and buys back duplicates.
RUNES.tip: A bigger box means more rings on hand to switch between. Visit Vasu often to identify your finds.

RUNES.name: Magic Potion
RUNES.from: Bought from Syrup's Potion Shop in the Sunken City (reachable in Winter via the snow bridge) for 300 Rupees, but only after you trade Syrup the Mushroom for the Wooden Bird to reopen her shop. Also found in Gasha Nuts / dropped by Maple.
RUNES.what: A one-use emergency revive — if Link runs out of hearts while carrying a potion, it's used automatically to refill his life instead of a Game Over.
RUNES.tip: Always carry one before a tough boss; it's your safety net. (Oracle of Seasons has no row of empty bottles like later games — the Magic Potion is the standalone life-saver.)

CHAMPIONS.name: Fertile Soil
CHAMPIONS.note: The first Essence of Nature, won from the Gnarled Root Dungeon (boss Aquamentus).

CHAMPIONS.name: Gift of Time
CHAMPIONS.note: The second Essence, won from Snake's Remains (boss Dodongo).

CHAMPIONS.name: Bright Sun
CHAMPIONS.note: The third Essence, won from Poison Moth's Lair (boss Mothula).

CHAMPIONS.name: Soothing Rain
CHAMPIONS.note: The fourth Essence, won from the Dancing Dragon Dungeon (boss Gohma).

CHAMPIONS.name: Nurturing Warmth
CHAMPIONS.note: The fifth Essence, won from Unicorn's Cave (boss Digdogger).

CHAMPIONS.name: Blowing Wind
CHAMPIONS.note: The sixth Essence, won from the Ancient Ruins (boss Manhandla).

CHAMPIONS.name: Seed of Life
CHAMPIONS.note: The seventh Essence, won from the Explorer's Crypt (boss Gleeok).

CHAMPIONS.name: Changing Seasons
CHAMPIONS.note: The eighth and last Essence, won from the Sword & Shield Maze — completing the Maku Seed.

ROADMAP.name: The Eight Essences of Nature
ROADMAP.note: One Essence waits in each of Holodrum's eight dungeons. Gather all eight and the Maku Tree forms the Maku Seed, your key to Onox's Castle.

ROADMAP.name: The Rod of Seasons
ROADMAP.note: Won from the four Season Spirits in Subrosia, the Rod lets you change the season at stumps — reshaping the land to open every path.

ROADMAP.name: Magic Rings
ROADMAP.note: Find ring boxes and Gasha-nut seeds, then have Vasu appraise them. Equip rings for powers — extra damage, defense, new abilities. There are 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Ages game).

ROADMAP.name: Pieces of Heart
ROADMAP.note: Twelve are hidden across Holodrum — some only reachable in a particular season.

TIPS.name: Changing the seasons
items: The Rod of Seasons (from Subrosia) changes Holodrum's season when you stand on a stump and swing it. Each season transforms the SAME map: Spring blooms flowers/vines to climb, Summer dries up water and grows vine ladders, Autumn raises mushrooms and clears leaves, Winter freezes water solid and heaps snow into ramps.
If a screen looks impassable, find the nearest season stump and try all four seasons — one of them opens the way.
You start only able to set ONE season; you earn the others from the Season Spirits as you progress.

TIPS.name: Subrosia & seeds
items: Portals (glowing holes) drop you into Subrosia, the fiery under-realm — home of the Temple of Seasons, the smithy (sword/shield upgrades), and trading sidequests.

Magic Seeds power your Slingshot and tools: Ember (fire/torches), Scent (lure & damage), Pegasus (speed), Gale (warp to seed trees), Mystery (random). Refill from the seed trees dotted around Holodrum.
Gasha Seeds planted in soft soil grow Gasha Nuts that hold rings, rupees, and items.

TIPS.name: Rings & the linked game
items: Collect ring boxes and have Vasu (in Horon Village) appraise unidentified rings. Equip one at a time for effects — the Power Ring boosts attack, the Armor Ring defense, and dozens more.

Oracle of Seasons and Oracle of Ages are a linked pair: beat one, then enter its password into the other to play a harder 'linked' quest, share rings, and unlock the TRUE final boss (Twinrova → Ganon).
A bottled Fairy or a Gasha Nut potion can save you in a tough dungeon.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Pieces of Heart
COLLECTIBLES.note: Every 4 make a full Heart Container — some only reachable in a particular season.

COLLECTIBLES.label: Magic Rings
COLLECTIBLES.note: Appraised by Vasu; equip one for a power. 64 in all (shareable with a linked Oracle of Ages).
